findings: Verified identity: Eastern Wood-Pewee (Contopus virens), family Tyrannidae.
All visible plate claims are supported: name, scientific name, family, behavior, woodland/edge habitat, field marks, and the stated 15–17 cm length, 25–28 cm wingspan, and 12–14 g weight.

The illustration matches the references and verified diagnosis: olive-gray upperparts, off-white throat/belly, dusky vest, small peaked head, long pointed wings, long narrow tail, two thin pale wingbars, minimal eye-ring, and a dark upper bill with yellow-orange lower mandible.
Anatomy is coherent: one head, one beak, two legs/feet, one tail, and a normal two-winged bird shown in lateral view with the far wing occluded. No extra or malformed body parts.

Minor stylistic variance only: the eye is enlarged and glossier than in the field-reference photos, but this does not obscure the species-appropriate lack of a prominent eye-ring or create a material identification error.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.

verification_sources.title: Eastern Wood-Pewee Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
